ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Fifth Amended Petition For Relief For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; Third-Party Liability For Breach Of Trust; And Return Of Property Filed By Petitioner Acela Cortese
Off Calendar Mar-02-2020 Continued To Jun-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
PTR-16-299823 In Re: Robert A. Naify
PTR-16-300479 The Francesca P. Naify Living Trust

Appearances:
Ben Riley for Co-Trustees, John Sherwood and Edward Topham.
Stacie Nelson and Jaime Herren for Petitioner, Christina Cortese.
Ciaran O'Sullivan for Petitioner, Acela Cortese.
Kimberly Famady for John Sherwood, as individual in the Francesca P. Naify Living Trust.

The Court encouraged parties to meet and confer to get issue of a discovery referee in place and for counsel to address the issue of consolidation of the MSJ and MSA.
Ms. Nelson stated that Mr. Riley's MSJ is set for April and her MSA is set for August, and requested that the motions be consolidated or coordinated somewhere in the middle, proposed that both motions be heard in May, that she would keep her opposition due date the same, and that both motions have common question of $1 billion. Mr. Riley stated that his motion was filed in November, was previously continued by the other side, that there would be no more continuances per the court, that there are separate issues, and requested to leave the April MSJ hearing date as is. The Court stated the hearing dates will remain as is.
Mr. Riley stated that he needs to take Christina's deposition in the Francesca case, that Christina lives in Spain and does not want to travel because of the corona-virus, and suggested a compromise to set a date and to split the travel cost if any changes were needed. Ms. Nelson stated that Mr. Riley can go to Spain or the deposition can be done by video, that it is fair that Christina is afraid to travel at this time, that the deposition can be taken in June, and it does not need to be set today. The Court noted that equity is not on her side if she isn't deposed, that she is the petitioner, it's her motion, and plans can be made and adjusted.
Ms. Nelson stated that Mr. Riley identified mediation as an option, that the 5-year rule to bring matters to trial is coming up next year, and asked whether this rule apply to probate. The Court stated to wait to see what happens with the Robert MSJ, that it expects the case to be tried early next year, and that it is being monitored closely.
Ms. Herren spoke of issues with getting filings conditionally under seal and requested a minute order. The Court noted it will not issue an order and tells counsel to follow the rules.

The Third Amended Petition for Relief, etc., (filed by Christine Cortese) and Fifth Amended Petition for Relief, etc., (filed by Acela Cortese are continued to June 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Updated joint status report to be filed by June 1, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-19-299823 In Re: Robert A. Naify and PTR-16-300479 The Francesca P. Naify Living Trust.

Appearances: Benjamin Riley for John Sherwood and Edward Topham (Co-Trustees).
Stacie Nelson for Christina Cortese (Petitioner).
James Lamping for Acela Cortese (Petitioner).
Robert Goodin for John Sherwood (in individual capacity).

The Court noted that it received the status statement and that it was very helpful. Ms. Nelson stated that there may be motions to compel to the extent they need to bring one if needed, that Judge Stewart was interested in receiving letter briefs rather than full motions and asked if Judge Quinn was interested in doing the same. The Court informed Counsel that discovery motions will need to take the normal course. Mr. Riley stated that he filed a motion for summary judgment and that he was unable to submit the thumb drive as the Clerk would not accept it. The Court stated that it would be helpful to have the opposition in the form of a flash drive and that Counsel can lodge it with the Court. Mr. Riley raised the issue of bringing the matter before Judge Stewart. The Court advised Counsel of the possibility of Judge Stewart's term ending in January. Mr. Riley stated that they have only filed two status conference statements, that he has been working very hard and that it makes sense to have a trial in 2021. The Court encouraged the parties to keep a target of trial in early 2021 and to consider having a Discovery Referee. Mr. Lamping informed the Court that one of his Client's petition is on appeal. Ms. Nelson stated that Christina has not discussed mediation. Mr. Lamping stated that they had mediation in Acela's case. Mr. Riley stated that they mediated in Acela's case in 2018, that there are some issues, that Christina will need to do motions and that Christina is not ready to mediate yet. Mr. Riley also raised the issue of bifurcation, stated that there are many investments that may be challenged, that he notified Counsel of his need to amend to add one more statute of limitations, that he did not hear back and that he may need to file motions. The Court orders the parties to meet and confer. Ms. Nelson noted that orders to amend that were filed many moons ago. The Sixth Amended Petition for Relief for Breach of Fiduciary Duty, etc., the First Amended Petition for Relief for Breach of Oral Agreement Promise of an Inheritance, etc., the Fifth Amended Petition for Relief for Fiduciary Duty, etc. and the Third Amended Petition for Relief for Breach of Fiduciary Duty, etc. are continued to March 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint status report is due by February 14, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299823 In Re: Robert A. Naify and PTR-16-300479 The Francesca P. Naify Trust.

Appearances: James Lamping and Ciarin O'Sullivan for Acela Cortese.
Stacie Nelson for Christina Cortese (Petitioner).
Kimberly Fanady and Robert Goodin for John Sherwood (Individually).
Ben Riley and Rob Bunzel for Co-Trustees.
